text:
The AI Inference boom is now driving demand across the full compute stack from silicon & memory to optics, power, packaging & cloud:

• AI Silicon: $NVDA, $AMD, $AVGO, $INTC
• Foundry & Packaging: $TSM, $AMKR, $GFS
• Memory & Storage: $MU, $SNDK, $WDC, $STX
• Cloud Platforms: $AMZN, $GOOGL, $MSFT, $BABA
• Power & Analog: $TXN, $ADI, $NXPI, $STM, $MPWR
• Optical Network: $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, $TSEM, $GLW, $MRVL, $AXTI

$NVDA has built one of the deepest strategic investment portfolios in public markets with bets across nearly every layer of the AI stack: 

• Physical AI: Figure AI, Wayve
• Nuclear Fusion: Commonwealth Fusion
• GPU cloud: $CRWV, $NBIS, Nscale, Lambda
• Chip Design & Manufacturing: $INTC, $SNPS, Groq
• Networking & optics: $LITE, $COHR, $MRVL, Enfabrica
• Foundation models: OpenAI, xAI, Mistral, Cohere, Perplexity

Nearly every investment either consumes Nvidia GPUs today or helps create demand for more of them tomorrow.

All portfolio performance and transaction data have been independently validated by an independent third party:

savvytrader.com/StockSavvySh…

A few methodology notes before you dig in:

• PEG ratio reflects each company’s valuation relative to its expected next 3 years of Non-GAAP EPS growth.
• 'Rule of 40' is typically calculated by combining the growth rate + operating margin to showcase a profitability adjusted growth metric.

Together the PEG ratio + Rule of 40 help show which software names are combining durable growth, margin quality & reasonable valuation.

I find them useful because strong trends often consolidate at these levels before continuing higher & this list reflects names consolidating between the 0.382  & 0.618 extension levels.

David Sacks says Anthropic is great at two things: launching products and scaring people.

He says they keep pairing new model releases with worst-case AI studies to market fear alongside the product.

Absolutely wild that Anthropic was founded less than five years ago is already devouring an entire software category and is worth more by market cap than all of these blue-chip SaaS companies combined:

• $CRM $154B
• $CRWD $96B
• $NOW $85B
• $SNOW $43B
AI cinema is going to be a real thing the next couple years

NASA’s Artemis II crew capsule has landed safely back on Earth.

The crew is about to be home.
$SNDK will join the Nasdaq-100 replacing $TEAM

Anthropic says Claude for Word is now in beta letting users draft, edit & revise documents directly from the sidebar while preserving formatting and tracked changes.

Claude can also pull context across $MSFT Word, Excel & PowerPoint in a single conversation.

$TSLA says FSD Supervised has been approved in the Netherlands and will begin rolling out there shortly.

The company says the system is trained on billions of real-world driving kilometers and can handle nearly any drive under supervision.

A U.S. official tells me the U.S. did not receive such a warning
Iran explicitly informed the Pakistani mediator that if the vessel continued its movement it would be targeted within 30 minutes and the Iran-US negotiations would be damaged.

bloomberg.com/news/articles/…

Interesting take by Bloomberg:

A US destroyer was seen moving from Fujairah toward the waterway, Iran’s semi-official Fars news agency said separately.

Iran’s armed forces monitored it and conveyed this to the US via Pakistani mediators, the news agency said.

The US vessel returned from the strait after Tehran warned that it would be targeted, according to Fars.

GEN. PETRAEUS: What I mean by Arsenal of Democracy is that Ukraine will be most important military-industrial complex in free world.

It is producing most impressive, cutting-edge, extraordinary unmanned systems, that integrate software with hardware, with constant innovation.

🚨🇺🇸🚢Several U.S. navy ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, U.S. official says
🚨🇺🇸🚢The move was not coordinated with Iran. It's the first time this happens since the beginning of the war

A couple of bold tankers making a run for it betting Iran will not attempt to stop them in the middle of the talks? Or, is the Strait finally opening up.

Unless this talks produce a deal in the next 24 to 48 hours, I’ll go with the former.

From what we can see, 133 tankers have departed the Strait of Hormuz over the past 41 days since 2026-03-01. That's only 3.24 tankers a day.

Of that total, 90 are known sanctions violators which wouldn't be paying any tolls to Iran.

Among the remaining 43 tankers, we see 3 VLCC supertankers, 1 LNG tanker and 27 LPG tankers.

Iraq's oil exports remain abysmal. So much for "brotherly" relations.

#OOTT #Tankers #IranWar

This map shows the future of oil.

@JavierBlas explains how the Strait of Hormuz could be changed forever 🎥

2/2

But no signs of shipping moving ... yet???

In our February report, we flagged early warning signs: Bitcoin, high-beta names, and the Magnificent 7 had each quietly put in a top, even as the major indices continued higher.

Since then, that weakness has spread as small caps and global markets have joined, also breaking critical support. We discuss what this means for investors in our analysis below. ⬇️

You don’t grow unless you suffer.

You will have friends and family who will say that’s not true.

They are wrong.

They are average.

The exceptional are exceptional because of pain - because they suffered.

They fought alone.

You must suffer.

Do not relent.

Persevere.

All politics is theatre.

The ceasefire will not last.

Bulls get fucked next week just like bears got fucked this week.

I’m right.

Goodnight.
